Go to the documentation of this file.

00024 #include "wrappers.h"
00025 
00026 #include "class.h"
00027 #include "header.h"
00028 #include "loader.h"
00029 #include "memory.h"
00030 #include "thread.h"
00031 #include "util.h"
00032 #include "vm.h"
00033 
00034 #include "java_lang_ref_WeakReference.h"
00035 
00036 /******************************************************************************
00037  * Type definitions                                                           *
00038  ******************************************************************************/
00039 
00042 struct finalizable_t {
00043     struct finalizable_t *next; 
00044     uintptr_t ref; 
00045 };
00046 
00048 typedef struct finalizable_t finalizable_t;
00049 
00052 struct small_chunk_t {
00053     struct small_chunk_t *next; 
00054 };
00055 
00057 typedef struct small_chunk_t small_chunk_t;
00058 
00061 struct large_chunk_t {
00062     struct large_chunk_t *next; 
00063     size_t size; 
00064 };
00065 
00067 typedef struct large_chunk_t large_chunk_t;
00068 
00070 #define BIN_ENTRIES (16)
00071 
00073 #define BIN_MAX_SIZE (BIN_ENTRIES * sizeof(jword_t))
00074 
00076 #define HEAP_INIT_FRACTION (16)
00077 
00079 #define TEMP_ROOT_INC (4)
00080 
00087 struct heap_t {
00088     bool collect; 
00089     size_t size; 
00090     size_t max_size; 
00091 
00092     void *memory; 
00093     uintptr_t start; 
00094     uintptr_t end; 
00095     uintptr_t perm; 
00096 
00097     uint8_t *bitmap; 
00098 
00099     java_lang_ref_WeakReference_t *weakref_list; 
00100 
00101     large_chunk_t *large_bin; 
00102     small_chunk_t *bin[BIN_ENTRIES]; 
00103 
00104 #if JEL_FINALIZER
00105     uintptr_t finalizer; 
00106     finalizable_t *finalizable; 
00107     finalizable_t *finalizing; 
00108 #endif // JEL_FINALIZER
00109 };
00110 
00112 typedef struct heap_t heap_t;
00113 
00114 /******************************************************************************
00115  * Local functions prototypes                                                 *
00116  ******************************************************************************/
00117 
00118 static uintptr_t gc_alloc(size_t);
00119 static void gc_purge_bin( void );
00120 static void gc_mark( void );
00121 static void gc_mark_finalizable( void );
00122 static void gc_sweep(size_t);
00123 static void gc_purge_weakref_list( void );
00124 static void gc_grow(uintptr_t, size_t);
00125 
00126 // Bitmap management functions
00127 
00128 static inline void bitmap_set(uintptr_t);
00129 static inline void bitmap_clear(uintptr_t);
00130 static inline bool bitmap_get(uintptr_t);
00131 
00132 // Chunk management functions
00133 
00134 static uintptr_t get_chunk(size_t);
00135 static void put_chunk(uintptr_t, size_t);
00136 
00137 #ifndef NDEBUG
00138 void print_bin( void );
00139 #endif // !NDEBUG
00140 
00141 /******************************************************************************
00142  * Local declarations                                                         *
00143  ******************************************************************************/
00144 
00146 static heap_t heap;
00147 
00148 /******************************************************************************
00149  * Heap implementation                                                        *
00150  ******************************************************************************/
00151 
00163 void gc_init(size_t size)
00164 {
00165     void *unified_heap;
00166     uint8_t *bitmap;
00167     size_t init_size;
00168     size_t heap_size; // Actual size of the heap in jwords
00169 
00170     // Grow the heap size if it is smaller than the canonical 32KiB CLDC heap
00171     size = (size < 32768) ? 32768 : size;
00172 
00173     /* Truncate the size to a multiple of 4 or 8 bytes, so we won't bother about
00174      * alignment problems, we assume that malloc() will already return a
00175      * properly aligned block */
00176     size = size_ceil(size, sizeof(jword_t));
00177     heap_size = (size * sizeof(jword_t) * 8) / ((sizeof(jword_t) * 8) + 1);
00178     heap_size = size_floor(heap_size, sizeof(jword_t));
00179     init_size = size_ceil(heap_size / HEAP_INIT_FRACTION, sizeof(jword_t));
00180 
00181     unified_heap = malloc(size);
00182     memset(unified_heap, 0, size);
00183     bitmap = (uint8_t *) unified_heap + heap_size;
00184 
00185     if (unified_heap == NULL) {
00186         dbg_error("Out of memory, cannot allocate the unified heap.");
00187         vm_fail();
00188     }
00189 
00190     // Initialize the heap structure
00191     heap.size = init_size;
00192     heap.max_size = heap_size;
00193     heap.memory = unified_heap;
00194     heap.start = (uintptr_t) unified_heap;
00195     heap.end = (uintptr_t) unified_heap + init_size;
00196     heap.perm = heap.start + heap_size;
00197     heap.weakref_list = NULL;
00198     heap.bitmap = bitmap;
00199     heap.large_bin = NULL;
00200 
00201     memset(heap.bin, 0, sizeof(small_chunk_t *) * BIN_ENTRIES);
00202 
00203     // Initialize the first free chunk
00204     put_chunk((uintptr_t) unified_heap, init_size);
00205 
00206     /* Disable the collector, we will re-enable it once all the other global
00207      * structures have been initialized */
00208     heap.collect = false;
00209 
00210 #if JEL_FINALIZER
00211     heap.finalizer = JNULL;
00212     heap.finalizable = NULL;
00213     heap.finalizing = NULL;
00214 #endif // JEL_FINALIZER
00215 } // gc_init()
00216 
00219 void gc_teardown( void )
00220 {
00221     free(heap.memory);
00222 } // gc_teardown()
00223 
00227 void gc_enable(bool enable)
00228 {
00229     heap.collect = enable;
00230 } // gc_enable()
00231 
00242 uintptr_t gc_new(class_t *cl)
00243 {
00244     uintptr_t ptr;
00245     header_t *header; // Pointer to the new object's header
00246     size_t size; // Size of the object (in words) to be allocated
00247     uint32_t ref_n;
00248 
00249     tm_lock();
00250 
00251 #if SIZEOF_VOID_P == (SIZEOF_JWORD_T / 2)
00252     ref_n = size_ceil(class_get_ref_n(cl), 2);
00253 #else
00254     ref_n = class_get_ref_n(cl);
00255 #endif
00256 
00257     size = (ref_n * sizeof(uintptr_t)) + sizeof(header_t)
00258            + size_ceil(class_get_nref_size(cl), sizeof(jword_t));
00259 
00260     assert((size >= sizeof(jword_t)) && (size % sizeof(jword_t) == 0));
00261 
00262     ptr = gc_alloc(size);
00263     ptr += ref_n * sizeof(uintptr_t); // Point to the header
00264     bitmap_set(ptr);
00265     header = (header_t *) ptr;
00266     *header = header_create_object(cl);
00267 
00268 #if JEL_PRINT
00269     if (opts_get_print_memory()) {
00270         fprintf(stderr, "NEW PTR: %p SIZE: %zu\n", (void *) ptr, size);
00271     }
00272 #endif // JEL_PRINT
00273 
00274     tm_unlock();
00275 
00276     return ptr;
00277 } // gc_new()
00278 
00284 uintptr_t gc_new_array_nonref(array_type_t type, int32_t count)
00285 {
00286     class_t *cl = bcl_array_class(type);
00287     size_t size;
00288     array_t *array;
00289     uintptr_t ptr;
00290 
00291     if (type == T_BOOLEAN) {
00292         size = size_div_inf(count, 8);
00293     } else {
00294         size = count * array_elem_size(type);
00295     }
00296 
00297     size = size_ceil(sizeof(array_t) + size, sizeof(jword_t));
00298     tm_lock();
00299     ptr = gc_alloc(size);
00300     bitmap_set(ptr);
00301     array = (array_t *) ptr;
00302     array->header = header_create_object(cl);
00303     array->length = count;
00304     tm_unlock();
00305 
00306 #if JEL_PRINT
00307     if (opts_get_print_memory()) {
00308         fprintf(stderr, "NEW PTR: %p SIZE: %zu\n", (void *) ptr, size);
00309     }
00310 #endif // JEL_PRINT
00311 
00312     return ptr;
00313 } // gc_new_array_nonref()
00314 
00320 uintptr_t gc_new_array_ref(class_t *cl, int32_t count)
00321 {
00322     ref_array_t *array;
00323     uintptr_t ptr;
00324     size_t size;
00325 
00326 #if SIZEOF_VOID_P == (SIZEOF_JWORD_T / 2)
00327     size = size_ceil(count, 2) * sizeof(uintptr_t);
00328 #else
00329     size = count * sizeof(uintptr_t);
00330 #endif // SIZEOF_VOID_P == (SIZEOF_JWORD_T / 2)
00331 
00332     tm_lock();
00333     ptr = gc_alloc(sizeof(ref_array_t) + size);
00334     ptr += size;
00335     bitmap_set(ptr);
00336     array = (ref_array_t *) ptr;
00337     array->header = header_create_object(cl);
00338     array->length = count;
00339     tm_unlock();
00340 
00341 #if JEL_PRINT
00342     if (opts_get_print_memory()) {
00343         fprintf(stderr, "NEW PTR: %p SIZE: %zu\n", (void *) ptr,
00344                 sizeof(ref_array_t) + size);
00345     }
00346 #endif // JEL_PRINT
00347 
00348     return ptr;
00349 } // gc_new_array_ref()
00350 
00357 uintptr_t gc_new_multiarray(class_t *cl, uint8_t dimensions, jword_t *counts)
00358 {
00359     uintptr_t *references;
00360     uintptr_t ref;
00361     int32_t count = *((int32_t *) counts);
00362     int32_t i;
00363 
00364     if (count == 0) {
00365         return JNULL;
00366     }
00367 
00368     if (dimensions == 1) {
00369         if (cl->elem_type != PT_REFERENCE) {
00370             return gc_new_array_nonref(prim_to_array_type(cl->elem_type),
00371                                        count);
00372         } else {
00373             return gc_new_array_ref(cl, count);
00374         }
00375     } else {
00376         ref = gc_new_array_ref(cl, count);
00377         references = array_ref_get_data((array_t *) ref);
00378 
00379         thread_push_root(&ref);
00380 
00381         for (i = 0; i < count; i++) {
00382             references[-i] = gc_new_multiarray(cl->elem_class, dimensions - 1,
00383                                                counts + 1);
00384         }
00385 
00386         thread_pop_root();
00387 
00388         return ref;
00389     }
00390 } // gc_new_multiarray()
00391 
00392 #if JEL_FINALIZER
00393 
00398 void gc_register_finalizer(uintptr_t ref)
00399 {
00400     heap.finalizer = ref;
00401 } // gc_register_finalizer()
00402 
00406 void gc_register_finalizable(uintptr_t ref)
00407 {
00408     finalizable_t *fin;
00409 
00410     thread_push_root(&ref);
00411     tm_lock();
00412     fin = gc_malloc(sizeof(finalizable_t));
00413     fin->ref = ref;
00414     fin->next = heap.finalizable;
00415     heap.finalizable = fin;
00416     thread_pop_root();
00417     tm_unlock();
00418 } // gc_register_finalizable()
00419 
00424 uintptr_t gc_get_finalizable( void )
00425 {
00426     thread_t *self = thread_self();
00427     uintptr_t ref = JNULL;
00428     finalizable_t *fin;
00429 
00430     monitor_enter(self, heap.finalizer);
00431 
00432     if (heap.finalizing == NULL) {
00433         thread_wait(heap.finalizer, 0, 0);
00434     }
00435 
00436     assert(heap.finalizing != NULL);
00437     fin = heap.finalizing;
00438     heap.finalizing = fin->next;
00439     monitor_exit(self, heap.finalizer);
00440     ref = fin->ref;
00441     gc_free(fin);
00442 
00443     return ref;
00444 } // gc_get_finalizable()
00445 
00446 #endif // JEL_FINALIZER
00447 
00452 void gc_register_weak_ref(java_lang_ref_WeakReference_t *ref)
00453 {
00454     tm_lock();
00455     ref->next = heap.weakref_list;
00456     heap.weakref_list = ref;
00457     tm_unlock();
00458 } // gc_register_weak_ref()
00459 
00468 void gc_grow(uintptr_t end, size_t size)
00469 {
00470     if (heap.end + size > heap.perm) {
00471         heap.end = heap.perm;
00472     } else {
00473         heap.end += size;
00474     }
00475 
00476     heap.size = heap.end - heap.start;
00477     put_chunk(end, heap.end - end);
00478 } // gc_grow()
00479 
00485 void gc_collect(size_t grow)
00486 {
00487     tm_lock();
00488 
00489 #if JEL_PRINT
00490     if (opts_get_print_memory()) {
00491        fprintf(stderr, "GARBAGE COLLECTION\n");
00492     }
00493 #endif // JEL_PRINT
00494 
00495     if (heap.collect != false) {
00496         tm_stop_the_world(); // Wait for all threads to stop
00497 
00498         // Mark garbage collected objects
00499         gc_mark();
00500         gc_mark_finalizable();
00501 
00502         // Mark and purge of non-garbage collected structures
00503         gc_purge_weakref_list();
00504         jsm_purge();
00505         tm_purge();
00506         gc_purge_bin();
00507 
00508         gc_sweep(grow);
00509     } else {
00510         gc_grow(heap.end, grow); // Just grow the heap
00511     }
00512 
00513     // If a collection happened this will also restart the stopped threads
00514     tm_unlock();
00515 } // gc_collect()
00516 
00521 size_t gc_free_memory( void )
00522 {
00523     small_chunk_t *schunk;
00524     large_chunk_t *lchunk;
00525     size_t size;
00526     uint32_t i;
00527 
00528     tm_lock();
00529     size = 0;
00530 
00531     for (i = 0; i < BIN_ENTRIES; i++) {
00532         schunk = heap.bin[i];
00533 
00534         while (schunk != NULL) {
00535             size += (i + 1) * sizeof(jword_t);
00536             schunk = schunk->next;
00537         }
00538     }
00539 
00540     lchunk = heap.large_bin;
00541 
00542     while (lchunk != NULL) {
00543         size += lchunk->size;
00544         lchunk = lchunk->next;
00545     }
00546 
00547     tm_unlock();
00548     return size;
00549 } // gc_free_memory()
00550 
00555 size_t gc_total_memory( void )
00556 {
00557     return heap.size;
00558 } // gc_total_memory()
00559 
00563 void gc_mark_potential(uintptr_t ref)
00564 {
00565     /* If it is not aligned to a JWORD_SIZE boundary then this is definetely
00566      * not a reference to a Java object */
00567     if (ref & (sizeof(jword_t) - 1)) {
00568         return;
00569     }
00570 
00571     // If it exceeds the heap bounds this is not a reference to a Java object
00572     if ((ref < heap.start) || (ref >= heap.end)) {
00573         return;
00574     }
00575 
00576     /* If the corresponding entry in the bitmap is not marked then this is not
00577      * a reference to a Java (or C) object */
00578     if (!bitmap_get(ref)) {
00579         return;
00580     }
00581 
00582     /* If we got here then we have something that may be a pointer and actually
00583      * points to something, let's gc_mark it */
00584     gc_mark_reference(ref);
00585 } // gc_mark_potential()
00586 
00591 #if !JEL_POINTER_REVERSAL
00592 
00593 void gc_mark_reference(uintptr_t ref)
00594 {
00595     class_t *cl;
00596     header_t *header, *new_header;
00597     uintptr_t *references;
00598     uintptr_t new_ref;
00599     uint32_t ref_n;
00600 
00601     if (ref == JNULL) {
00602         return;
00603     }
00604 
00605     header = (header_t *) ref;
00606 
00607     if (!header_is_object(header)) {
00608         return;
00609     }
00610 
00611     cl = header_get_class(header);
00612 
00613     if (header_is_marked(header)) {
00614         return;
00615     } else {
00616         header_set_mark(header);
00617     }
00618 
00619     if (class_is_array(cl) && (cl->elem_type == PT_REFERENCE)) {
00620         ref_n = array_get_ref_n((array_t *) header);
00621     } else {
00622         ref_n = class_get_ref_n(cl);
00623     }
00624 
00625     references = ((uintptr_t *) header) - ref_n;
00626 
00627     for (size_t i = 0; i < ref_n; i++) {
00628         new_ref = references[i];
00629 
00630         if (new_ref == JNULL) {
00631             continue;
00632         } else {
00633             new_header = (header_t *) new_ref;
00634 
00635             if (header_is_marked(new_header)) {
00636                 continue;
00637             } else {
00638                 gc_mark_reference(new_ref);
00639             }
00640         }
00641     }
00642 } // gc_mark_reference()
00643 
00644 #else
00645 
00646 void gc_mark_reference(uintptr_t ref)
00647 {
00648     class_t *cl;
00649     uintptr_t prev, curr, tmp;
00650     header_t *header, *tmp_header;
00651     jword_t count;
00652     uint32_t ref_n;
00653     bool ref_array;
00654 
00655     if (ref == JNULL) {
00656         return;
00657     }
00658 
00659     header = (header_t *) ref;
00660 
00661     if (!header_is_object(header) || header_is_marked(header)) {
00662         return;
00663     }
00664 
00665     curr = ref;
00666     prev = JNULL;
00667 
00668     header = (header_t *) curr;
00669     header_create_gc_counter(header);
00670 
00671     while (1) {
00672         cl = bcl_get_class_by_id(header_get_class_index(header));
00673 
00674         if (class_is_array(cl) && (cl->elem_type == PT_REFERENCE)) {
00675             ref_array = true;
00676             ref_n = ((array_t *) header)->length;
00677         } else {
00678             ref_array = false;
00679             ref_n = class_get_ref_n(cl);
00680         }
00681 
00682         count = header_get_count(header, ref_array);
00683 
00684         if (count < ref_n) {
00685             // We're not done yet with the current object
00686             tmp = *((uintptr_t *) curr - count - 1);
00687 
00688             if (tmp == JNULL) {
00689                 // Null reference, skip to the next object
00690                 header_set_count(header, count + 1, ref_array);
00691                 continue;
00692             }
00693 
00694             tmp_header = (header_t *) tmp;
00695 
00696             if (!header_is_marked(tmp_header)) {
00697                 /* This object is not marked, gc_mark it, push the previous
00698                  * object and set this one as the current */
00699                 *((uintptr_t *) curr - count - 1) = prev;
00700                 prev = curr;
00701                 curr = tmp;
00702                 header = tmp_header;
00703                 header_create_gc_counter(header);
00704                 continue;
00705             } else {
00706                 // This object has already been marked, skip to the next one
00707                 header_set_count(header, count + 1, ref_array);
00708                 continue;
00709             }
00710         } else {
00711             // We're done with the current object, pop the previous
00712             tmp = curr;
00713             curr = prev;
00714             header = (header_t *) curr;
00715 
00716             // We returned to the root pointer, return to the caller
00717             if (curr == JNULL) {
00718                 return;
00719             }
00720 
00721             cl = bcl_get_class_by_id(header_get_class_index(header));
00722 
00723             if (class_is_array(cl) && (cl->elem_type == PT_REFERENCE)) {
00724                 ref_array = true;
00725             } else {
00726                 ref_array = false;
00727             }
00728 
00729             count = header_get_count(header, ref_array);
00730             prev = *((uintptr_t *) curr - count - 1);
00731             *((uintptr_t *) curr - count - 1) = tmp;
00732             header_set_count(header, count + 1, ref_array);
00733         }
00734     }
00735 } // gc_mark_reference()
00736 
00737 #endif // !JEL_POINTER_REVERSAL
00738 
00745 static uintptr_t gc_alloc(size_t size)
00746 {
00747     uintptr_t ptr = get_chunk(size);
00748 
00749     if (ptr == 0) {
00750         // get_chunk() failed... collect and retry
00751         gc_collect(size);
00752         ptr = get_chunk(size);
00753 
00754         if (ptr == 0) {
00755             dbg_error("Out of memory. Try giving the VM a larger heap with the"
00756                       " --size <size_in_bytes> option.");
00757             vm_fail();
00758         }
00759     }
00760 
00761     memset((jword_t *) ptr, 0, size);
00762     return ptr;
00763 } // gc_alloc()
00764 
00771 static void gc_purge_bin( void ) {
00772     small_chunk_t *schunk;
00773     large_chunk_t *lchunk;
00774     char *free_space;
00775     size_t free_size;
00776 
00777     // Empty the fixed-size bins
00778 
00779     for (size_t i = 0; i < BIN_ENTRIES; i++) {
00780         schunk = heap.bin[i];
00781         heap.bin[i] = NULL;
00782 
00783         while (schunk != NULL) {
00784             free_space = (char *) schunk;
00785             free_size = (i + 1) * sizeof(jword_t);
00786             schunk = schunk->next;
00787             memset(free_space, 0, free_size); // Clear the fred space
00788         }
00789     }
00790 
00791     // Empty the large bin
00792     lchunk = heap.large_bin;
00793     heap.large_bin = NULL;
00794 
00795     while (lchunk != NULL) {
00796         free_space = (char *) lchunk;
00797         free_size = lchunk->size;
00798         lchunk = lchunk->next;
00799         memset(free_space, 0, free_size); // Clear the fred space
00800     }
00801 } // gc_purge_bin()
00802 
00813 static void gc_mark( void )
00814 {
00815     bcl_mark();
00816     jsm_mark();
00817     tm_mark();
00818 } // gc_mark()
00819 
00825 static void gc_mark_finalizable( void )
00826 {
00827 #if JEL_FINALIZER
00828     thread_t *self = thread_self();
00829     finalizable_t *curr = heap.finalizable;
00830     finalizable_t *prev = NULL;
00831     finalizable_t *next;
00832 
00833     while (curr != NULL) {
00834         if (!header_is_marked((header_t *) (curr->ref))) {
00835             // Add the object to the queue of the objects being finalized
00836             next = curr->next;
00837             curr->next = heap.finalizing;
00838             heap.finalizing = curr;
00839 
00840             if (curr->next == NULL) {
00841                 monitor_enter(self, heap.finalizer);
00842                 thread_notify(heap.finalizer, false);
00843                 monitor_exit(self, heap.finalizer);
00844             }
00845 
00846             if (prev == NULL) {
00847                 heap.finalizable = next;
00848             } else {
00849                 prev->next = next;
00850             }
00851 
00852             curr = next;
00853         } else {
00854             prev = curr;
00855             curr = curr->next;
00856         }
00857     }
00858 
00859     // Resurrect the objects which are being finalized
00860     curr = heap.finalizing;
00861 
00862     while (curr != NULL) {
00863         gc_mark_reference(curr->ref);
00864         curr = curr->next;
00865     }
00866 #endif // JEL_FINALIZER
00867 } // gc_mark_finalizable()
00868 
00875 static void gc_sweep(size_t size)
00876 {
00877     class_t *cl;
00878     header_t *header;
00879     uintptr_t scan = heap.start;
00880     uintptr_t start; // Start of a new object
00881     uintptr_t end = heap.start; // End of the previous object
00882     size_t reclaimed = 0;
00883     size_t in_use = 0;
00884     size_t max_size = 0;
00885     size_t nref_size, ref_n;
00886     bool is_java;
00887 #if JEL_POINTER_REVERSAL
00888     uint32_t id;
00889 #endif // JEL_POINTER_REVERSAL
00890 
00891     while (scan < heap.end) {
00892         if ((*((uintptr_t *) scan) & ((1 << HEADER_RESERVED) - 1)) == 0) {
00893             scan += sizeof(jword_t);
00894 
00895             if (scan >= heap.end) {
00896                 break;
00897             } else {
00898                 continue;
00899             }
00900         }
00901 
00902         header = (header_t *) scan; // We found a header
00903 
00904         if (header_is_object(header)) {
00905             assert(bitmap_get(scan));
00906             is_java = true;
00907 
00908 #if JEL_POINTER_REVERSAL
00909             if (header_is_marked(header)) {
00910                 id = header_get_class_index(header);
00911                 header_restore(header, bcl_get_class_by_id(id));
00912                 header_set_mark(header);
00913             }
00914 #endif // JEL_POINTER_REVERSAL
00915 
00916             cl = header_get_class(header);
00917 
00918             if (class_is_array(cl)) {
00919                 nref_size = array_get_nref_size((array_t *) header);
00920                 ref_n = array_get_ref_n((array_t *) header);
00921             } else {
00922                 nref_size = class_get_nref_size(cl);
00923                 ref_n = class_get_ref_n(cl);
00924             }
00925         } else {
00926             is_java = false;
00927             ref_n = 0;
00928             nref_size = header_get_size(header);
00929         }
00930 
00931         nref_size = size_ceil(nref_size, sizeof(jword_t));
00932 #if SIZEOF_VOID_P == (SIZEOF_JWORD_T / 2)
00933         ref_n = size_ceil(ref_n, 2);
00934 #endif // SIZEOF_VOID_P == (SIZEOF_JWORD_T / 2)
00935 
00936         if (header_is_marked(header)) {
00937             if (is_java) {
00938                 header_clear_mark(header);
00939             }
00940 
00941             start = scan - ref_n * sizeof(uintptr_t);
00942 
00943             /* If there was some free space between the previous object and
00944              * this one reclaim it */
00945             if (start - end >= sizeof(jword_t)) {
00946                 if (start - end > max_size) {
00947                     max_size = start - end;
00948                 }
00949 
00950                 put_chunk(end, start - end);
00951                 reclaimed += start - end;
00952             }
00953 
00954             // Skip to the next object
00955             scan += sizeof(header_t) + nref_size;
00956             end = scan;
00957             in_use += ref_n * sizeof(uintptr_t) + sizeof(header_t) + nref_size;
00958         } else {
00959             // This is a dead object remove it from the bitmap
00960             assert(header_is_object(header));
00961             bitmap_clear(scan);
00962             scan += sizeof(header_t) + nref_size;
00963         }
00964     }
00965 
00966     if (heap.end - end > max_size) {
00967         max_size = heap.end - end;
00968     }
00969 
00970     reclaimed += heap.end - end;
00971 
00972     // Check if we have fred enough memory, otherwise we grow the heap
00973     if ((max_size > size) && (reclaimed > in_use / 2)) {
00974         put_chunk(end, heap.end - end);
00975     } else {
00976         if (reclaimed < in_use / 2) {
00977             size = size_max(size, in_use / 2 - reclaimed);
00978             size = size_ceil(size, sizeof(jword_t));
00979         }
00980 
00981         gc_grow(end, size);
00982     }
00983 
00984 #if JEL_PRINT
00985     if (opts_get_print_memory()) {
00986        fprintf(stderr, "GARBAGE COLLECTION in_use = %zu reclaimed = %zu\n",
00987                in_use, reclaimed);
00988     }
00989 #endif // JEL_PRINT
00990 } // gc_sweep()
00991 
01002 void *gc_malloc(size_t size)
01003 {
01004     uintptr_t ptr;
01005     header_t *header;
01006 
01007     if (size == 0) {
01008         return NULL;
01009     }
01010 
01011     size = size_ceil(size, sizeof(jword_t)) + sizeof(header_t);
01012     tm_lock();
01013     ptr = gc_alloc(size);
01014     header = (header_t *) ptr;
01015     *header = header_create_c(size - sizeof(header_t));
01016 
01017 #if JEL_PRINT
01018     if (opts_get_print_memory()) {
01019        fprintf(stderr, "MALLOC PTR: %p SIZE: %zu bytes\n",
01020                (void *) (ptr + sizeof(jword_t)), size - sizeof(header_t));
01021     }
01022 #endif // JEL_PRINT
01023 
01024     tm_unlock();
01025 
01026     return (void *) (ptr + sizeof(header_t));
01027 } // gc_malloc()
01028 
01037 void *gc_palloc(size_t size)
01038 {
01039     uintptr_t ptr = 0;
01040 
01041     if (size == 0) {
01042         return NULL;
01043     }
01044 
01045     size = size_ceil(size, sizeof(jword_t));
01046     tm_lock();
01047 
01048     if (heap.perm - size >= heap.end) {
01049         heap.perm -= size;
01050         ptr = heap.perm;
01051         memset((jword_t *) ptr, 0, size);
01052     }
01053 
01054     tm_unlock();
01055 
01056     // Fallback to a normal allocation if we're out of permanent space
01057     if (ptr == 0) {
01058         ptr = (uintptr_t) gc_malloc(size);
01059     }
01060 
01061 #if JEL_PRINT
01062     if (opts_get_print_memory()) {
01063        fprintf(stderr, "PALLOC PTR: %p SIZE: %zu bytes\n", (void *) ptr, size);
01064     }
01065 #endif // JEL_PRINT
01066 
01067     return (void *) ptr;
01068 } // gc_palloc()
01069 
01076 void gc_free(void *ptr)
01077 {
01078     if (ptr == NULL) {
01079         return;
01080     }
01081 
01082     assert(((uintptr_t) ptr >= heap.start) && ((uintptr_t) ptr < heap.perm));
01083 
01084     tm_lock();
01085     header_t *header = (header_t *) ((uintptr_t) ptr - sizeof(header_t));
01086 
01087 #ifdef JEL_PRINT
01088     if (opts_get_print_memory()) {
01089        fprintf(stderr, "FREE PTR: %p SIZE = %zu bytes\n",
01090                ptr, header_get_size(header));
01091     }
01092 #endif // JEL_VERBOSE_GC
01093 
01094     put_chunk((uintptr_t) header, header_get_size(header) + sizeof(header_t));
01095     tm_unlock();
01096 } // gc_free()
01097 
01102 static void gc_purge_weakref_list( void )
01103 {
01104     java_lang_ref_WeakReference_t *curr, *prev, list;
01105     header_t *referent;
01106 
01107     prev = &list;
01108     prev->next = NULL;
01109     curr = heap.weakref_list;
01110 
01111     while (curr != NULL) {
01112         if (header_is_marked(&(curr->header))) {
01113             /*
01114              * This weak reference is marked, if the referent is marked too
01115              * we can leave it alone, otherwise the referent is only weakly
01116              * reacheable so we have to clear the weak reference pointing to it
01117              */
01118             referent = (header_t *) curr->referent;
01119 
01120             if (!header_is_marked(referent)) {
01121                 curr->referent = JNULL;
01122             }
01123 
01124             prev = curr;
01125         } else {
01126             // This reference is not marked, remove it from the queue
01127             prev->next = curr->next;
01128         }
01129 
01130         curr = curr->next;
01131     }
01132 
01133     heap.weakref_list = list.next;
01134 } // heap_purge_weakref_list()
01135 
01139 static inline void bitmap_set(uintptr_t ptr)
01140 {
01141     uintptr_t offset = (ptr - heap.start) / sizeof(jword_t);
01142 
01143     heap.bitmap[offset >> 3] |= 1 << (offset & 0x7);
01144 } // bitmap_set()
01145 
01149 static inline void bitmap_clear(uintptr_t ptr)
01150 {
01151     uintptr_t offset = (ptr - heap.start) / sizeof(jword_t);
01152 
01153     heap.bitmap[offset >> 3] &= ~(1 << (offset & 0x7));
01154 } // bitmap_clear()
01155 
01159 static inline bool bitmap_get(uintptr_t ptr)
01160 {
01161     uintptr_t offset = (ptr - heap.start) / sizeof(jword_t);
01162 
01163     return (heap.bitmap[offset >> 3] >> (offset & 0x7)) & 1;
01164 } // bitmap_get()
01165 
01170 static uintptr_t get_chunk(size_t size)
01171 {
01172     small_chunk_t *schunk;
01173     large_chunk_t *lchunk, *curr, *prev;
01174     uintptr_t res;
01175     uint32_t id;
01176 
01177     if (size <= BIN_MAX_SIZE) {
01178         // Try finding an exact match
01179         id = (size / sizeof(jword_t)) - 1;
01180 
01181         while (id < BIN_ENTRIES) {
01182             if (heap.bin[id] != NULL) {
01183                 schunk = heap.bin[id];
01184                 heap.bin[id] = schunk->next;
01185                 res = (uintptr_t) schunk;
01186                 put_chunk(res + size, (id + 1) * sizeof(jword_t) - size);
01187                 return res;
01188             } else {
01189                 id++;
01190             }
01191         }
01192 
01193         // Try finding a larger chunk
01194         if (heap.large_bin != NULL) {
01195             // We found a larger block, chop it and return the relevant part
01196             lchunk = heap.large_bin;
01197             heap.large_bin = lchunk->next;
01198             res = (uintptr_t) lchunk;
01199             put_chunk(res + size, lchunk->size - size);
01200             return res;
01201         }
01202     } else {
01203         // Find an appropriate block in the large chunk list
01204         prev = NULL;
01205         curr = heap.large_bin;
01206 
01207         while (curr != NULL) {
01208             if (curr->size >= size) {
01209                 if (prev) {
01210                     prev->next = curr->next;
01211                 } else {
01212                     heap.large_bin = curr->next;
01213                 }
01214 
01215                 res = (uintptr_t) curr;
01216                 put_chunk(res + size, curr->size - size);
01217 
01218                 return res;
01219             } else {
01220                 prev = curr;
01221                 curr = curr->next;
01222             }
01223         }
01224     }
01225 
01226     return 0;
01227 } // get_chunk()
01228 
01234 static void put_chunk(uintptr_t chunk, size_t size)
01235 {
01236     uint32_t id;
01237     small_chunk_t *schunk;
01238     large_chunk_t *lchunk;
01239 
01240     assert(size % sizeof(jword_t) == 0);
01241 
01242     if (size == 0) {
01243         return;
01244     } else if (size <= BIN_MAX_SIZE) {
01245         id = (size / sizeof(jword_t)) - 1;
01246         schunk = (small_chunk_t *) chunk;
01247         schunk->next = heap.bin[id];
01248         heap.bin[id] = schunk;
01249     } else {
01250         lchunk = (large_chunk_t *) chunk;
01251         lchunk->next = heap.large_bin;
01252         lchunk->size = size;
01253         heap.large_bin = lchunk;
01254     }
01255 } // put_chunk()
01256 
01257 #ifndef NDEBUG
01258 
01261 void print_bin( void )
01262 {
01263     large_chunk_t *lchunk;
01264     small_chunk_t *schunk;
01265 
01266     fprintf(stderr, "print_bin()\n");
01267 
01268     for (size_t i = 0, j; i < BIN_ENTRIES; i++) {
01269         schunk = heap.bin[i];
01270         j = 0;
01271 
01272         while (schunk != NULL) {
01273             j++;
01274             schunk = schunk->next;
01275         }
01276 
01277         fprintf(stderr, "heap->bin[size = %zu] = %zu\n",
01278                 (i + 1) * sizeof(jword_t), j);
01279     }
01280 
01281     fprintf(stderr, "heap->large_bin = \n");
01282     lchunk = heap.large_bin;
01283 
01284     while (lchunk != NULL) {
01285         fprintf(stderr, "\tsize = %zu\n", lchunk->size);
01286         lchunk = lchunk->next;
01287     }
01288 
01289    fprintf(stderr, "\n");
01290 } // print_bin()
01291 
01292 #endif // !NDEBUG
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:26 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
